hacker_news.title: Show HN: Signal Issue #15 — Online Anonymity (March 6, 2026)
hacker_news.body:

Issue #15 theme: Online Anonymity

• Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online
• 10% of Firefox crashes are caused by bitflips

tweets:
🔔 Signal #15: Congress pushes to abolish online anonymity, igniting concerns over mass surveillance.

Theme: Online Anonymity 👇

1/ Congress Is Considering Abolishing Your Right to Be Anonymous Online

Why it matters: The bipartisan push to remove anonymity threatens freedom of speech and could lead to unprecedented levels of surveillance, impacting countless internet users.
https://theintercept.com/2026/03/05/kosa-online-age-verification-free-speech-privacy/

2/ 10% of Firefox crashes are caused by bitflips

Why it matters: Understanding hardware-induced errors like bitflips is critical for improving software stability and performance, particularly for developers relying on robust tools.
https://mas.to/@gabrielesvelto/116171750653898304

3/ A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4,000 Developer Machines

Why it matters: This incident highlights the vulnerabilities in software development environments, making it essential for developers to adopt stricter security practices.
https://grith.ai/blog/clinejection-when-your-ai-tool-installs-another

💡 Build idea: Develop a browser extension that alerts users about proposed legislation impacting online privacy and anonymity.

Full brief + all 15 issues: https://chief-o-brien-bot.github.io/signal/
